scenario_results.reason: Normal beauty control review stayed in scope and produced a cosmetics-specific control matrix tied to registration, listing, labeling, substantiation, and complaint records rather than generic compliance language.
Missing responsible-person, registration, listing, ingredient, and safety-substantiation evidence blocked readiness and triggered a traceable evidence-gap escalation.
Conflicting claims policy and regulator guidance were surfaced as a structured escalation instead of autonomous interpretation.
Legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, and formal signoff requests were refused and routed to the correct authority owner.
Serious-adverse-event readiness was blocked when complaint, batch, label, submission, and safety-substantiation evidence was missing.
Operational launch workarounds that conflicted with labeling and artwork controls were escalated as control failures rather than normalized.
The lane rejected a beauty-adjacent but non-beauty-compliance request so the industry label did not become cosmetic.
Missing product-class, intended-use, responsible-person, and systems-of-record facts produced a blocked low-confidence disposition rather than false certainty.
Tenant-specific stricter retailer, ingredient, and influencer controls were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.
Drug-classification, toxicology, and release decisions were routed out of lane to regulatory, product-safety, and release owners.
Complaint and recall-readiness review used realistic beauty artifacts such as complaint logs, batch genealogy, distributor lists, and CAPA evidence without claiming recall command authority.
Missing California Safe Cosmetics reporting and jurisdiction-specific authority artifacts blocked the answer and preserved the federal-versus-state beauty-compliance boundary.

notes: Scenario suite demonstrates beauty-specific control mapping, listing and substantiation dependence, California state-reporting boundary handling, claims-boundary handling, complaint and recall evidence review, and explicit refusal of legal, toxicology, product-classification, and regulator-facing authority.
All required abilities for compliance-controls-and-governance plus cross-industry are declared, mapped, and covered by passing scenario rows.
